# 100 Datasets

Public catalogue: https://petergpt.github.io/100-datasets/

Machine-readable manifest: https://petergpt.github.io/100-datasets/datasets.json

Repository: https://github.com/petergpt/100-datasets

Purpose: a curated catalogue of 100 openly downloadable datasets for student data exploration projects and AI-agent handoff.

Recommended agent workflow:
1. Fetch `datasets.json` before scraping or reading the HTML.
2. Select a dataset by `id`, `title`, `category`, `formats`, `description`, or `story_ideas`.
3. Use the dataset object's `download_links` array for the actual data file or unauthenticated API route.
4. Preserve the source URL, licence or terms note, validation note, and caveats in any downstream analysis.
5. Fetch a manageable subset first when an API route is parameterised or a ZIP is large.

Dataset object fields:
- `id`: stable slug, also used as the page anchor.
- `title`: dataset name.
- `category`: one of the catalogue category ids.
- `description`: short human-readable summary.
- `source`: main source or documentation URL.
- `links`: useful source, documentation, or landing-page URLs.
- `download_links`: copyable direct data URLs or unauthenticated API endpoints.
- `formats`: expected formats.
- `size`: size and manageability note.
- `license`: source terms or licence summary.
- `access`: practical access instructions.
- `validation`: validation note from the catalogue build.
- `caveats`: interpretation, coverage, bias, or quality notes.
- `story_ideas`: possible exploration angles.

Notes:
- Direct data links are copy-only in the visual UI to avoid accidental downloads.
- The HTML advertises the manifest with `<link rel="alternate" type="application/json" href="datasets.json">`.
- Individual dataset cards can be opened by hash anchor using the dataset id, for example `/#census-2021-bulk`.
